ransomware.name: Ransomware Response
steps: ISOLATE: Immediately disconnect affected systems from network (unplug ethernet, disable WiFi)
PRESERVE: Do NOT reboot or shut down — capture RAM dump first if possible
IDENTIFY: Determine ransomware family using file extension or ransom note (Crypto Sheriff, ID Ransomware)
SCOPE: Check what network shares, backups, and cloud drives are accessible from infected system
CONTAIN: Block C2 IP/domains at firewall, disable affected AD accounts
NOTIFY: Alert management, legal, and insurance — check if notification laws apply (GDPR, HIPAA)
RECOVER: Restore from clean, offline backups after full eradication
DOCUMENT: Preserve all evidence before cleanup for forensic analysis
iocs_to_look_for: Outbound connections to Tor or unknown IPs

phishing.name: Phishing Incident Response
steps: TRIAGE: Determine if link was clicked or attachment opened
CONTAIN: Reset credentials of affected user immediately
ANALYZE: Extract email headers, links, and attachments safely (use sandbox)
SCOPE: Check email gateway for same sender/subject to other employees
BLOCK: Add sender domain/IP to blocklist, submit URL to threat intel
INVESTIGATE: Review browser history, DNS queries, and process execution logs
REMEDIATE: Run AV/EDR full scan, check for persistence mechanisms
TRAIN: Report to user awareness team for training update

bruteforce.name: Brute Force Attack Response
steps: DETECT: Confirm attack via authentication logs (failed logins, login velocity)
CONTAIN: Temporarily block source IP(s) at firewall/WAF
ASSESS: Check if any login succeeded — if yes, treat as active compromise
LOCK: Enforce account lockout policy if not already active
HUNT: Check for successful logins from same IP range or at unusual hours
HARDEN: Enable MFA on targeted accounts, increase password complexity
MONITOR: Set alerts for continued attempts from new IPs (attacker may rotate)
REPORT: Log attack details for threat intelligence sharing
iocs_to_look_for: High volume of Event ID 4625 (failed Windows login)
SSH login attempts from single IP
Logins from Tor exit nodes or VPN ranges

dataexfil.name: Data Exfiltration Response
steps: IDENTIFY: Determine what data was accessed — DLP alerts, access logs, SIEM
CONTAIN: Immediately revoke access tokens, rotate API keys, block destination IPs
PRESERVE: Capture network flows, proxy logs, and endpoint telemetry
SCOPE: Identify all files/records accessed, downloaded, or transmitted
NOTIFY: Assess legal notification requirements (GDPR 72hr window, state breach laws)
HUNT: Look for persistence mechanisms — exfil often follows a dwell period
REMEDIATE: Patch the exploited vulnerability or misconfiguration
REVIEW: Audit access controls, implement DLP policies if absent
iocs_to_look_for: Large outbound data transfers at odd hours

lateral.name: Lateral Movement Response
steps: MAP: Build timeline of attacker movement across systems using SIEM
ISOLATE: Segment affected network zone, block inter-VLAN communication
RESET: Rotate all credentials on compromised systems and downstream accounts
HUNT: Check for Pass-the-Hash, Pass-the-Ticket, or Kerberoasting artifacts
REVIEW: Audit privileged account usage and service account activity
CONTAIN: Disable compromised accounts, revoke Kerberos tickets (krbtgt reset)
PATCH: Address the initial access vulnerability that enabled lateral movement
MONITOR: Deploy honeypots and enhanced logging on high-value targets

ddos.name: DDoS Attack Response
steps: DETECT: Confirm traffic spike via network monitoring and bandwidth graphs
CONTACT: Notify your ISP/CDN provider for upstream filtering
ANALYZE: Identify attack vector (SYN flood, UDP amplification, HTTP flood, etc.)
MITIGATE: Enable rate limiting, deploy anti-DDoS appliances, activate CDN protection
FILTER: Block attacking IPs at perimeter, implement geo-blocking if applicable
SCALE: Activate auto-scaling for cloud resources if available
MONITOR: Track attack duration and intensity, watch for pivot to other attack types
DOCUMENT: Log attack characteristics for future defense tuning
iocs_to_look_for: High UDP/ICMP flood from multiple sources

insiderthreat.name: Insider Threat Response
steps: ASSESS: Determine if threat is malicious or negligent
PRESERVE: Immediately capture user activity logs before they're rotated
CONTAIN: Suspend user account access without alerting the individual
INVESTIGATE: Review file access, email, USB usage, cloud uploads, print logs
LEGAL: Involve HR and legal counsel before taking further action
RECOVER: Identify what data was accessed or exfiltrated
PREVENT: Implement DLP, privileged access management, user behavior analytics
REVIEW: Audit access controls and separation of duties policies
iocs_to_look_for: Large file downloads outside business hours
Access to files outside user's normal scope
USB device usage or cloud storage uploads

webdefacement.name: Web Defacement Response
steps: ISOLATE: Take affected web server offline or put in maintenance mode
PRESERVE: Capture full disk image and web server logs before restoration
IDENTIFY: Determine attack vector (SQL injection, file upload, compromised credentials)
SCOPE: Check if defacement is only visual or if backdoors were planted
RESTORE: Revert to known-good backup or redeploy from source control
PATCH: Fix the vulnerability that allowed the defacement
SCAN: Run malware scanner and web shell detector on entire web root
MONITOR: Enable enhanced logging and file integrity monitoring
iocs_to_look_for: New PHP/ASPX web shells in upload directories
Suspicious POST requests in web server logs
